# Test: Class initialization with library catalog system
# Features: __init__, instance fields, instance methods, multiple classes

class Book:
    title: str
    author: str
    pages: int
    is_available: bool

    def __init__(self, title: str, author: str, pages: int):
        self.title = title
        self.author = author
        self.pages = pages
        self.is_available = True

    def checkout(self) -> None:
        self.is_available = False

    def return_book(self) -> None:
        self.is_available = True

    def get_status(self) -> str:
        if self.is_available:
            return "Available"
        else:
            return "Checked Out"


class Library:
    name: str
    total_books: int

    def __init__(self, name: str):
        self.name = name
        self.total_books = 0

    def add_book(self) -> None:
        self.total_books += 1

    def get_count(self) -> int:
        return self.total_books


def main():
    book1 = Book("1984", "Orwell", 328)
    print(book1.title)
    print(book1.pages)
    print(book1.get_status())
    
    book1.checkout()
    print(book1.get_status())
    
    lib = Library("City Library")
    lib.add_book()
    lib.add_book()
    print(lib.get_count())

# EXPECTED OUTPUT:
# 1984
# 328
# Available
# Checked Out
# 2
